summary: LOAD-BEARING: §4.2.2 vs §4.2.1 distinguishing case. Mixed-type keyset where bytewise-on-encoded-key-bytes (§4.2.2) produces a different sort than length-first (§4.2.1). Profile spec Example 3 / V10.
notes: Under §4.2.2: int key 100 encodes as 18 64 (2 bytes), empty tstr as 60 (1 byte). Bytewise compare 0x18 vs 0x60 — int sorts first. Under §4.2.1 (length-first, NON-CONFORMANT): empty tstr (1 byte) sorts first, giving a26060 6162 18 64 6161 — the opposite order. Any runtime claiming §4.2.2 conformance MUST pass this case.
